title: arnica spathulata var. eastwoodiae
intro: Arnica spathulata var. eastwoodiae is a rare and endemic plant species, also known as Eastern Arnica, which belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Arnica spathulata var. eastwoodiae include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting skin health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, salves, and creams, which are often used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
